note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/Divisors.lean:399 defines Nat.Perfect n := (sum of proper divisors = n) ∧ 0<n for all n, with no parity restriction — the general notion an 'odd perfect number' would instantiate (Odd n ∧ Nat.Perfect n); no odd-specific lemma or nonexistence result exists.

Mathlib/Order/Heyting/Basic.lean:276-278, docstring literally reads 'The deduction theorem in the Heyting algebra model of intuitionistic logic: an implication holds iff the conclusion follows from the hypothesis' (theorem: a ⇨ b = ⊤ ↔ a ≤ b). Only the Heyting-algebra semantic instance, not the general syntactic deduction theorem for a Hilbert-style calculus (Mathlib has no proof-theoretic Provable/Hilbert-system framework).
